message:
feat(core): fresh MCP config creation, URL normalization, KPI threshold tuning, and lock tracking fix

MCP config (mcp_config.rs):
- Add fresh-install MCP config creation support: build_new_server_entry,
  insert_server_entry_text, create_fresh_config_text, setup_mcp_config_file,
  and preferred_config_path functions. These allow the installer and setup
  flow to create or augment MCP config files for all supported tools
  (Claude, Codex, Cursor, Gemini, Copilot, Windsurf, Cline, OpenCode,
  FactoryDroid) without requiring an existing Python entry.
- Comprehensive test coverage for the new config creation paths.

Setup (setup.rs):
- Normalize unspecified bind addresses (0.0.0.0, ::, [::]) to 127.0.0.1
  in client-facing URLs, preventing agents from connecting to the
  unroutable wildcard address.
- Add flexible URL matching for status checks: urls_match_for_status
  compares scheme, host, port, and normalized path. Treats /api/ and
  /mcp/ paths as equivalent for backward compatibility.
- Full URL parsing and normalization helpers with tests.

KPI (kpi.rs):
- Fix anomaly score mapping: ratio [0.5, 2.0] now maps to score [0.0, 1.0]
  (was [0.5, 1.0] range), giving more granular severity differentiation.
- Simplify severity thresholds: >= 2.0 = Critical, >= 1.0 = High,
  >= 0.75 = Medium, else Low.

Metrics (metrics.rs):
- Add tui_spin_watchdog_trips_total, tui_spin_watchdog_last_cpu_pct_x100,
  and tui_spin_watchdog_last_trip_us to SystemMetrics and snapshot.

Lock order (lock_order.rs):
- Fix did_release to use rposition search instead of pop-and-assert,
  allowing correct release tracking when locks are released out of
  strict LIFO order (which can happen with concurrent async code).

Pattern overlap (pattern_overlap.rs):
- Change mismatched segment count heuristic from "assume disjoint" to
  "assume overlap" (conservative fallback) to prevent false negatives.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
